question: [File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `NotFoundError` thrown by `getScheduleById()` when `rows.length !== 1` to perform schedule ID enumeration — distinguishing between non-existent IDs (404) and existing schedules — and build a complete list of valid schedule IDs for subsequent analysis of staking-reward-triggering transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user issue concurrent requests to `getSchedules()` with different `account.id` filters to simultaneously enumerate schedules for multiple target accounts, building a real-time map of all pending staking-reward-triggering schedules across the network without triggering per-account rate limits?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractSqlFromScheduleFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `applicableFilters` counter in `extractSqlFromScheduleFilters()` — which only counts `ACCOUNT_ID` and `SCHEDULE_ID` filters — to observe that `LIMIT` and `ORDER` filters are processed separately and do not affect the `whereQuery`, and use this to craft requests that retrieve maximum data with minimum observable filter complexity, evading anomaly detection while enumerating staking reward schedules?
